Title: My DISK
Notes:
Upon receiving reports of degraded performance on the client's MyDisk environment, I responded promptly and initiated a thorough investigation. The reported symptoms included significant response latency, delays in file opening, and disruptions to file synchronization — all of which were directly impacting the client's day-to-day business operations.

I took immediate action by performing an initial standard reboot of the MyDisk system, which yielded minor improvements. Recognizing that the issue persisted, I escalated my approach and executed a full clean reboot of the environment to ensure a complete system refresh and eliminate any lingering processes contributing to the degradation.

Despite the clean reboot showing normal load metrics from my monitoring perspective, I acknowledged the client's continued experience of lag and proactively engaged the vendor to conduct a deeper root-cause analysis and committed to delivering a long-term, sustainable solution — not just a temporary fix.

Following continued monitoring, I identified resource utilization running below 20%, ruling out capacity overload as the primary cause and narrowing the scope of investigation.

Additionally, I swiftly addressed a separate Cisco Umbrella access concern raised by the client, which was resolved promptly without any impact to operations. The client want us to check with the issue tonight and make sure this issue wont persist long. Giving it to next team.
